name: Genoise
recipeIngredient: 100 grams Sweet Butter; Melted and Clarified
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and lightly flour one of the following baking pans:
- Two 9-inch layer cake tins
- Three 7-inch layer cake tins
- One 11x16 jelly-roll pan
- Two shallow 10-inch layer-cake tins
2. Preheat oven to 180°C.
3. In a large bowl, combine eggs and sugar. Beat for about one minute, or until they are just combined.
4. Set the bowl over a saucepan containing 3-5 cm of hot water. The water should not touch the bowl or boil.
5. Place the saucepan with the bowl over low heat for 5-10 minutes, stirring gently to prevent cooking at the bottom of the bowl.
6. When the eggs feel lukewarm and resemble bright yellow syrup, remove the bowl from the heat. Beat on high speed for 10-15 minutes, scraping with a rubber spatula when necessary, until the mixture becomes light, fluffy, and cool (it will almost triple in volume and look like whipped cream).
7. Sprinkle flour over the beaten eggs, then gently fold in slightly cooled clarified butter and vanilla extract. Folding can be done using an electric mixer on the lowest speed or by hand.
8. Pour batter into prepared pans and bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until cakes pull away from the sides of the pan and are golden brown when touched on top.
9. Remove from pans immediately and cool on a cake rack.
Variations:
To modify the basic Genoise recipe, consider one of the following options:
Lemon or Orange Genoise
Add 1 teaspoon grated orange or lemon rind and 2 tablespoons orange or lemon juice to the melted butter before adding it to the batter.
Genoise for Petits Fours
Make the Genoise slightly firmer by increasing flour to 180 grams. Bake in a 30x40 cm jelly-roll pan.
Nutted Genoise
Add 75 grams finely grated nuts when incorporating the flour into the batter.
Chocolate Genoise
Substitute 60 grams unsweetened cocoa for 60 grams of the flour.
